tool.name: degapseq
tool.summary: Remove '-' and '.' gap characters from sequence records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `degapseq` removes gap markers from sequence records and emits the normalized residue stream through the shared EMBOSS-RS sequence model. The v1 implementation is intentionally literal: only `-` and `.` are stripped, record ordering is preserved, and no alignment-aware coordinate bookkeeping is attempted.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current tool accepts local sequence inputs through the governed sequence IO path. Multi-record inputs are supported. FASTA is the primary exercised format in the current validation path, while other sequence formats depend on the shared IO readers.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: Output is a normalized sequence collection rendered through the shared result and CLI layers. Identifiers, descriptions, and preserved record metadata remain attached to each transformed sequence record.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs degapseq`. Validation currently covers deterministic gap removal against a committed two-record FASTA fixture, and the Rust service path is exercised against the same repository-managed input.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release strips only the explicit gap characters `-` and `.`. It does not preserve alignment column structure or emit an annotated alignment result, so it should be treated as a sequence-normalization step rather than as alignment editing.

artifacts.label: Two-record gapped FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA fixture containing explicit `-` and `.` gap markers for deterministic degapseq validation.

examples.title: Remove explicit gap markers from a committed FASTA fixture
examples.description: Runs `degapseq` against a repository-managed two-record FASTA fixture and removes `-` and `.` while preserving record order.

expected_outputs.label: Gap-free sequence output
expected_outputs.description: Stable FASTA output with `ACGT` and `TTA` as the normalized residues for the two fixture records.
